architectureFigure.caption: Figure 2: The RecZero and RecOne frameworks utilize a unified target to optimize multi-step reasoning within a coupled structure, achieving reasoning-enhanced predictions specifically designed for recommendation scenarios.

authors: Xiaoyu Kong, Junguang Jiang, Bin Liu, Ziru Xu, Han Zhu, Jian Xu, Bo Zheng, Jiancan Wu, Xiang Wang

breakthroughAssessment.justification: The paper presents a strong contribution by introducing an RL-based paradigm for LLM-enhanced recommendation, effectively addressing key limitations of existing distillation methods. RecZero's autonomous reasoning and rule-based rewards, coupled with RecOne's hybrid SFT+RL approach, demonstrate significant performance gains and cost-effectiveness. The work is highly useful to the sub-field of LLM-based recommenders and RL applications. It provides a novel direction for improving reasoning capabilities in LLMs for personalized recommendations. The practical benefits in terms of efficiency and adaptability are notable.

coreProblem.concreteExample: General-purpose teacher LLMs often lack domain-specific knowledge for recommendation, producing reasoning traces that are misaligned with the rate prediction objective. Additionally, generating high-quality reasoning data (whether via human annotation or LLM API calls) is time- and resource-intensive, and fine-tuned student models may only imitate surface-level reasoning patterns.
coreProblem.statement: Existing distillation-based methods for leveraging LLMs' reasoning capabilities in rating prediction suffer from limitations such as insufficient recommendation capability of teacher models, costly and static supervision, and superficial transfer of reasoning ability.
whyItMatters: Suboptimal reasoning processes in teacher models hinder the student model's performance.
High costs for data generation and static supervision limit the scalability and adaptability of LLM-based recommenders.
Superficial transfer of reasoning ability leads to overfitting and poor generalization to unseen tasks, limiting the practical applicability of these models.

evaluationHighlights: RecOne achieved significant performance improvements, lowering RMSE by 6.7% on Book, 12.2% on Music, and 6.2% on Yelp, and cutting MAE by 16.8% on Book, 29.9% on Music, and 7.5% on Yelp, compared to the strongest LLM-based baseline (Reason4Rec).
RecZero (pure RL) also surpassed all baselines in terms of MAE across the three datasets, demonstrating the effectiveness of the RL paradigm even without cold-start initialization.

RecZero(early-stop) achieved a 21.5% MAE reduction against the strongest pure-SFT baseline (Reason4Rec) with significantly fewer labeled samples (0.48K vs. 20K) and computational resources, demonstrating superior cost-effectiveness.
The cold-start SFT initialization in RecOne proved effective in enhancing the model's initial capabilities and elevating its performance ceiling for RL optimization, achieving faster convergence.
Ablation studies confirmed the critical impact of proposed components such as structured multi-step thinking and rule-based reward modeling on recommendation performance.

evaluationSetup.setting: Rating prediction task on real-world datasets

experimentFigures.caption: Figure 3: The performance of RecZero and RecOne over training steps.
Figure 4: 4a and 4b separately show the MAE and RMSE performance of RecOne and its four ablation variants on four datasets.
Figure 4: 4a and 4b separately show the MAE and RMSE performance of RecOne and its four ablation variants on four datasets.

keyResultGroups.title: RecZero vs. Strongest LLM-based Baseline (Reason4Rec) on MAE

mainTakeaways: RecOne significantly outperforms all baseline methods, including traditional and LLM-based approaches, across all evaluated metrics (RMSE and MAE) and datasets.
The pure RL approach (RecZero) also surpasses all baselines in terms of MAE, demonstrating the inherent superiority of the RL paradigm for autonomous reasoning-enhanced recommendation.
RecOne's cold-start supervised fine-tuning (SFT) initialization effectively boosts the model's initial capabilities and elevates its performance ceiling during subsequent RL optimization.
The proposed components, including the structured 'Think-before-Recommendation' prompt and rule-based reward modeling, are crucial for achieving the superior performance of RecOne.

RecZero and RecOne offer a simpler and more cost-efficient pipeline (single model, teacher-free, end-to-end optimization) compared to complex multi-stage distillation methods, requiring fewer labeled samples, less training time, and lower inference cost.
Both RecZero and RecOne enable fast adaptation to cold-start scenarios and distribution shifts with minimal new interactions, making them well-suited for dynamic commercial recommender systems.

institutions: Taobao & Tmall Group of Alibaba, China; National University of Singapore; Institute of Dataspace, Hefei Comprehensive National Science Center; Shanghai Key Laboratory of Data Science

explanation: RecZero introduces a pure Reinforcement Learning (RL) paradigm for LLM-based recommendation, which trains a single LLM end-to-end to autonomously develop reasoning capabilities for rating prediction, departing from traditional multi-model and multi-stage distillation approaches.

The 'Think-before-Recommendation' prompt construction employs a structured reasoning template with special tokens (e.g., <analyze user>, <analyze item>, <match>, <rate>) to guide the LLM through step-wise analysis of user interests, item features, and user-item compatibility.

Rule-based Reward Modeling, utilizing Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), computes rewards for reasoning trajectories based on format correctness and ground-truth rating differences, optimizing the LLM without requiring costly labeled reasoning traces.

RecOne is a hybrid paradigm that combines cold-start supervised fine-tuning (SFT) with RL, using high-quality reasoning samples to initialize the LLM, thereby achieving faster convergence and stronger initial reasoning capabilities before further RL optimization.
keyNovelty.main: A novel RL-based paradigm (RecZero) for LLM-enhanced recommendation that enables autonomous reasoning and eliminates multi-model, multi-stage distillation, complemented by a hybrid approach (RecOne) for robust performance.

keyTerms.definition: The task of predicting a user's rating for a target item, often enhanced by explicit reasoning steps performed by large language models.

A common paradigm in LLM-based recommendation where a larger 'teacher' model generates intermediate reasoning traces or outputs, which are then used to supervise fine-tuning a smaller 'student' model.

A reinforcement learning policy optimization algorithm that reduces memory requirements by estimating group scores and replaces traditional reward models with rule-based reward mechanisms, guiding policy optimization by the relative performance of generated outputs within a group.
The problem in recommender systems where there is insufficient data (e.g., interactions, ratings) for new users or items, making it difficult to generate accurate recommendations.

comparisonToPriorWork: RecZero and RecOne address key limitations of existing distillation-based LLM recommendation methods, specifically, the teacher model's insufficient recommendation capability, costly and static supervision, and superficial transfer of reasoning ability.

Unlike multi-model, multi-stage distillation approaches (e.g., Rec-SAVER, EXP3RT, Reason4Rec), RecZero trains a single LLM end-to-end through pure RL, eliminating the need for separate teacher-student models and decoupled training stages.

RecZero's 'Think-before-Recommendation' prompt construction and rule-based reward modeling provide recommendation-specific objectives and autonomous optimization, contrasting with general-purpose teacher LLMs that may lack domain-specific knowledge or require manual label generation.

RecOne's hybrid approach, combining cold-start supervised fine-tuning (SFT) with subsequent RL, offers faster convergence and stronger initial capabilities than pure RL, and more robust reasoning than pure SFT, which often memorizes rather than genuinely generalizes.
limitations: Due to computational constraints, the study could not fully assess the potential performance gains achievable by leveraging larger base models for RL, potentially hindering a thorough evaluation of the models' true performance potential.
The study did not explore whether RecZero and RecOne could serve as viable replacements for existing Teacher models in generating cold-start data for multi-round self-iterative optimization.

modeling.label: Rating Prediction Rule
modeling.value: $\hat{y}_{u,i} = \text{argmax}_{k \in Y} P_\theta (y_{u,i} = k | H_u, M_i)$, where $\hat{y}_{u,i}$ is the predicted rating for user $u$ on item $i$, and $\theta$ represents the model parameters. For LLM: $\hat{r}_{u,i}, \hat{y}_{u,i} = \text{LLM}(x_{u,i}) = \text{LLM}(H_u, M_i)$, where $\hat{r}_{u,i}$ is the reasoning trace.

modeling.label: Format Reward
modeling.value: $R_{format} = \begin{cases} 0.5 & \text{if the format is correct} \\ -0.5 & \text{if the format is incorrect} \end{cases}$

modeling.label: GRPO Objective Function
modeling.value: $J_{GRPO}(\theta) = E_{x \sim D, \{y_i\}^G_{i=1} \sim \pi_{\theta_{old}}(\cdot|x)}[\frac{1}{G} \sum_{i=1}^G \frac{1}{|y_i|} \sum_{t=1}^{|y_i|} (\min(\frac{\pi_\theta(y_{i,t}|x, y_{i,<t})}{\pi_{\theta_{old}}(y_{i,t}|x, y_{i,<t})} \hat{A}_{i,t}, \text{clip}(\frac{\pi_\theta(y_{i,t}|x, y_{i,<t})}{\pi_{\theta_{old}}(y_{i,t}|x, y_{i,<t})}, 1 - \epsilon, 1 + \epsilon) \hat{A}_{i,t}) - \beta D_{KL}[\pi_\theta \| \pi_{ref}])]$

pipelineFlow: RecZero takes user history and target item as input to a single LLM.

The LLM performs structured reasoning through 'Think-before-Recommendation' prompts: user interest extraction (<analyze user>), item attribute summarization (<analyze item>), compatibility evaluation (<match>), and final rating prediction (<rate>).
Rule-based rewards (based on format correctness and accuracy of predicted rating) are computed from the generated reasoning traces and ratings.
Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is then used to refine the LLM's policy based on these rewards, optimizing reasoning and prediction end-to-end.

RecOne initiates its model with cold-start supervised fine-tuning (SFT) using high-quality reasoning traces generated by a teacher LLM (DeepSeek-R1), which are categorized into aligned and misaligned traces.
The SFT-initialized RecOne model is subsequently further optimized using the RecZero RL paradigm to enhance its reasoning capabilities and recommendation performance.

problemDefinition.label: Goal
problemDefinition.value: To predict user ratings for items that align with user preferences by training a large language model to autonomously develop reasoning capabilities.

technicalDetails.reproducibility: Yes, the paper fully discloses all necessary information for reproducibility in the Appendix, including details about datasets, experimental setups, hyperparameters, and evaluation metrics.

systemModules.details: A Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-1M model is used as the base LLM, serving as the core component that performs reasoning and rating prediction.
systemModules.name: Large Language Model (LLM)
systemModules.role: Core model for reasoning and prediction

systemModules.details: Employs a structured reasoning template with specific tags (<analyze user>, <analyze item>, <match>, <rate>) to guide the LLM's step-wise analysis of user interests, item features, and user-item compatibility.
systemModules.name: 'Think-before-Recommendation' Prompt Constructor
systemModules.role: Guides LLM to generate structured reasoning

systemModules.details: Adopts Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to compute rewards based on both format correctness ($R_{format}$) and the difference between predicted and ground-truth ratings ($R_{answer}$), promoting stable and efficient learning without external labels.
systemModules.name: Rule-based Reward Modeling

systemModules.details: A specialized sampling method to obtain high-quality reasoning traces, generated by a teacher LLM (DeepSeek-R1), which are used to initialize RecOne via supervised fine-tuning, especially for cold-start scenarios.
systemModules.name: Cold-Start Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT)
systemModules.role: Initializes RecOne with foundational reasoning capabilities

thesis: This paper proposes RecZero, an RL-based recommendation paradigm that trains a single LLM to autonomously develop reasoning capabilities for rating prediction, overcoming limitations of traditional distillation-based methods such as insufficient recommendation capability, costly supervision, and superficial transfer of reasoning ability. It also introduces RecOne, a hybrid paradigm combining SFT with RL for faster convergence and stronger performance.
title: Think before Recommendation: Autonomous Reasoning-enhanced Recommender
